Monday.theme: Welcome, Belonging, and Learning Our Classroom

blocks.title: Arrival and Breakfast
blocks.objective: Enter, unpack, eat, clean, and begin morning work.
blocks.teacher: Model each arrival step and praise exact behaviors.
blocks.success: Students begin morning work with limited prompting.

blocks.title: Morning Meeting and Community
blocks.objective: Learn carpet expectations, listening, speaking, and belonging routines.
blocks.teacher: Read First Day Jitters and begin the Class Promise.
blocks.students: Introduce themselves and name one way to help classmates feel welcome.
blocks.success: Students transition to the carpet and participate respectfully.

blocks.title: Literacy Routine Practice
blocks.objective: Practice attention signals, book care, partner roles, and reading stamina without Open Court.
blocks.teacher: Model correct and incorrect examples, then rehearse short cycles.
blocks.students: Practice getting books, partner talk, and three to five minutes of reading stamina.
blocks.success: Students respond to the signal and handle books responsibly.

blocks.title: Lunch and Recess Procedures
blocks.objective: Practice line, hallway, cafeteria, playground, and return routines.
blocks.teacher: Model every transition before students practice it.
blocks.students: Rehearse safe movement and signal response.
blocks.success: Students move safely and respond to adult signals.

blocks.title: Writing Tools and All About Me
blocks.objective: Learn pencil, supply, notebook, and sharing procedures.
blocks.teacher: Model how to request, use, and return materials.
blocks.students: Complete an All About Me drawing and sentence.
blocks.success: Students use and return supplies correctly.

blocks.title: Math Routine Practice
blocks.objective: Learn whiteboard, manipulative, partner, and cleanup routines without Eureka.
blocks.teacher: Run a low-risk number game and timed cleanup rehearsal.
blocks.students: Take turns, explain one idea, and return materials.
blocks.success: Students use math tools safely and clean up efficiently.

blocks.title: Science Safety and Classroom Tour
blocks.objective: Learn science safety and the purpose of classroom spaces.
blocks.teacher: Lead a classroom tour and connect room care to Heart.
blocks.students: Identify each area and practice using it.
blocks.success: Students name one expectation for each major classroom area.

blocks.title: Pack-Up and Dismissal
blocks.objective: Learn folder, backpack, dismissal-group, and waiting procedures.
blocks.teacher: Model each step and rehearse dismissal changes.
blocks.students: Pack and move to the correct location.
blocks.success: Students pack and dismiss safely.

Tuesday.theme: Rules, Expectations, Voice Levels, and School Tour

blocks.title: Independent Arrival Practice
blocks.objective: Complete arrival with fewer prompts.
blocks.teacher: Observe and reteach only when needed.
blocks.success: Most students begin independently.

blocks.title: Rules and Expectations
blocks.objective: Explain how rules keep everyone safe and ready to learn.
blocks.teacher: Read A Letter from Your Teacher and co-create positively worded rules.
blocks.success: Students explain one rule and its purpose.

blocks.title: Voice Levels and Literacy Routines
blocks.objective: Use the correct voice level for whole group, partners, and independent work.
blocks.teacher: Model and rehearse rapid changes between voice levels.
blocks.students: Match activities to voice levels and practice reading routines.
blocks.success: Students change voice level when signaled.

blocks.title: School Tour
blocks.objective: Identify important school locations and safe movement expectations.
blocks.teacher: Lead the tour and model hallway expectations.
blocks.students: Walk safely and identify where to get help.
blocks.success: Students name key locations and expectations.

blocks.title: Writing Goals
blocks.objective: Use writing procedures while naming one second-grade goal.
blocks.teacher: Model a simple goal sentence.
blocks.success: Students complete and submit a short task.

blocks.title: Math Partner Roles
blocks.objective: Practice taking turns, asking questions, and productive struggle.
blocks.teacher: Model helpful math-talk stems.
blocks.success: Partners share materials and take turns.

blocks.title: Rights, Responsibilities, and Consequences
blocks.objective: Connect classroom rights to responsibilities and logical consequences.
blocks.teacher: Teach consequences as calm opportunities to learn and repair.
blocks.success: Students name a logical response to one scenario.

blocks.title: Pack-Up Accuracy
blocks.objective: Improve pack-up while staying calm and accurate.
blocks.teacher: Time the routine without creating pressure.
blocks.success: Students improve without rushing unsafely.

reflection: Which rule helps our class most?
What tour location should we review?

Wednesday.theme: Teamwork, Transitions, and Peaceful Problem Solving

blocks.title: Arrival Challenge
blocks.objective: Complete arrival calmly and on time.
blocks.teacher: Use positive narration.
blocks.success: The class begins with minimal prompting.

blocks.title: Team Building and Problem Solving
blocks.objective: Use respectful words to solve small problems.
blocks.teacher: Model scenarios and respectful disagreement.
blocks.success: Students use a taught step.

blocks.title: Transition Boot Camp
blocks.objective: Move among carpet, desks, partners, and independent work safely.
blocks.teacher: Model, practice, reflect, and repeat.
blocks.success: Transitions become calm and efficient.

blocks.title: Recess Conflict Practice
blocks.objective: Practice inclusion, compromise, walking away, and asking for help.
blocks.teacher: Model playground scenarios.
blocks.success: Students identify peaceful solutions.

blocks.title: Writing Stamina
blocks.objective: Write independently and manage supplies.
blocks.teacher: Set a short stamina goal.
blocks.success: Students sustain writing.

blocks.title: Cooperative Math Challenge
blocks.objective: Use partner roles and encouragement.
blocks.teacher: Assign roles and monitor teamwork.
blocks.success: Students share tools and ideas.

blocks.title: Classroom Jobs and Observation Challenge
blocks.objective: Understand jobs and practice notice/wonder observations.
blocks.teacher: Introduce jobs and model observation language.
blocks.students: Practice jobs and record one notice and wonder.
blocks.success: Students explain how jobs help the class.

blocks.title: Dismissal Changes
blocks.objective: Respond safely when dismissal plans change.
blocks.teacher: Simulate a change.
blocks.students: Practice moving to the correct group.
blocks.success: Students remain calm and accurate.

reflection: When should we ask an adult for help?

blocks.title: Independent Arrival
blocks.objective: Complete arrival using visual cues only.
blocks.teacher: Use the visual rather than repeated directions.
blocks.success: Students complete most steps independently.

blocks.title: Reflection and Repair
blocks.objective: Understand how to reflect, repair, and try again after mistakes.
blocks.teacher: Model a correction conversation.
blocks.success: Students describe how to try again.

blocks.title: Independent Literacy Sequence
blocks.objective: Complete literacy routines with reduced support.
blocks.teacher: Post visual directions and monitor.
blocks.success: Students need minimal prompting.

blocks.title: Common-Area Independence
blocks.objective: Apply hallway, lunch, and recess expectations consistently.
blocks.teacher: Give brief feedback after transitions.
blocks.success: Students identify what went well.

blocks.title: Independent Writing Process
blocks.objective: Plan, write, illustrate, and turn in a short response.
blocks.teacher: Confer rather than direct every step.
blocks.success: Students finish independently.

blocks.title: Independent Math Routine
blocks.objective: Complete setup, partner work, checking, and cleanup.
blocks.teacher: Reduce prompting and observe.
blocks.success: Students manage materials safely.

blocks.title: Mini Investigation and Responsibility Scenarios
blocks.objective: Follow a full investigation routine and solve classroom scenarios.
blocks.teacher: Model one short investigation.
blocks.students: Complete the routine and discuss responsibility.
blocks.success: Students follow safety and cleanup expectations.

blocks.title: Independent Pack-Up
blocks.objective: Pack and dismiss with minimal reminders.
blocks.teacher: Use visual cues unless safety requires intervention.
blocks.success: Students are accurate and calm.

reflection: How do we repair a mistake?
What needs one more practice tomorrow?

Friday.theme: Full Schedule Practice, Celebration, and Readiness

blocks.title: Full Arrival Routine
blocks.objective: Complete arrival independently.
blocks.teacher: Observe and celebrate growth.
blocks.success: The class starts calmly.

blocks.title: Class Promise and Compliment Circle
blocks.objective: Reflect on belonging and finalize class commitments.
blocks.teacher: Read Oh, the Places You'll Go! and facilitate reflection.
blocks.students: Give a specific compliment and affirm the promise.
blocks.success: Students name how they will contribute.

blocks.title: Full Literacy Rehearsal
blocks.objective: Practice the entire literacy sequence without core curriculum.
blocks.teacher: Run a mock literacy block with familiar texts.
blocks.success: Students complete the sequence.

blocks.title: Lunch and Recess Independence
blocks.objective: Demonstrate common-area expectations.
blocks.teacher: Observe and reteach only as needed.
blocks.success: Students return ready to learn.

blocks.title: Successful Second Grader Reflection
blocks.objective: Identify one strength and one goal.
blocks.teacher: Model a reflective response.
blocks.success: Students identify a strength and goal.

blocks.title: Math Routine Celebration
blocks.objective: Demonstrate math setup, teamwork, and cleanup through a game.
blocks.teacher: Run a cooperative review game.
blocks.success: Students manage tools and teamwork.

blocks.title: Science Safety and Community Celebration
blocks.objective: Review safety and classroom citizenship without formal curriculum.
blocks.teacher: Review scenarios and celebrate growth.
blocks.success: Students explain expectations for Monday.

blocks.title: Final Pack-Up and Dismissal Practice
blocks.objective: Demonstrate end-of-day independence.
blocks.teacher: Celebrate specific improvements.
blocks.success: Students are ready for core instruction week.

reflection: How will you show Heart next week?
What will help our class learn successfully Monday?
